notes: The American Indian Studies major is designed to offer a coherent and comprehensive curriculum in American Indian cultures, societies, and contemporary issues in addition to valuable background in more traditional disciplines such an anthropology, art history, economics, education, history, law, linguistics, literature, sociology, and world arts and cultures. Students acquire a critical knowledge of the concepts, theories, and methods that have produced knowledge about American Indians in the traditional disciplines. Listed below are the lower division preparation courses for the major. To be considered for this major, you must complete the following coursework by the end of spring before transfer: One introductory course in American Indian Studies and Two additional preparatory courses in Sociological Anthropology, American Politics, Statistics and Introduction to Gender Studies. All courses must be taken for a letter grade. For more information regarding this major and UCLA's transfer selection process, visit https:// amindian.ucla.edu and https:// a dmission.ucla.edu . PLEASE NOTE: the community college courses listed below have been approved to satisfy the preparation requirements for this major at UCLA, but they may not be exact equivalents of the UCLA courses listed.
